item.title: Speech translation in Google Meet is now rolling out to mobile devices 摘要
item.description:
Google Meet is rolling out speech translation on mobile devices, allowing real-time translation between English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, and Italian. The feature uses AI to translate spoken language and outputs text in the user's preferred language with a voice imitation of the original speaker. Currently in alpha, it worked between two laptops but failed between an iPhone and iPad.
标签: Google, Google Meet, speech translation, real-time translation, AI, mobile, alpha, language

item.title: Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause 摘要
item.description:
Microsoft and OpenAI have renegotiated their partnership, effectively removing the clause that would nullify Microsoft's IP rights upon achieving AGI. Originally defined in OpenAI's charter as 'highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work,' AGI was later given a financial definition tied to $100 billion in profit. In October 2025, an independent expert panel was introduced to verify AGI. The latest agreement, announced April 27, 2026, extends Microsoft's non-exclusive license through 2032, ends revenue sharing from Microsoft, and makes payments from OpenAI to Microsoft independent of technological progress, effectively killing the AGI clause.
标签: AI, AGI, Microsoft, OpenAI, partnership, contract, technology, business, computer-history

item.title: Yours Truly on The Vergecast 摘要
item.description:
David Pierce and Nilay Patel are joined by John Gruber on The Vergecast to discuss Apple's leadership transition and Tim Cook's legacy. They debate whether Cook should be blamed for the Touch Bar's failure or for not iterating on it. The author argues Apple's biggest mistake was abandoning the Touch Bar after one attempt, calling the return to function keys uncharacteristically lazy.
标签: Apple, Tim Cook, Touch Bar, leadership, product design, The Vergecast, John Gruber, tech criticism

item.title: The Loop: everything has happened before, and everything will happen again 摘要
item.description:
The article argues that human history is driven by recurring cycles—financial bubbles, strongman politics, scapegoating, and technological disruption—rooted in unchanging Paleolithic brain wiring. It traces bubbles from tulip mania to AI stocks, showing how greed, social conformity, and risk miscalculation repeat. Strongmen emerge when institutions fail and uncertainty triggers a desire for authority. Scapegoating serves as social cohesion through collective violence against innocent victims. Even transformative inventions like the printing press initially amplify conflict. The core thesis: the human 'operating system' remains unpatched, causing the same errors across centuries.
标签: history, psychology, finance, bubbles, politics, strongman, scapegoating, technology, human nature, cycles
